title: Sour Flaky Middle Eastern Chicken Tagine
description: A tangy, flaky chicken tagine cooked in one pot, finished with a sprinkle of caramelized toffee popcorn for a sweet crunch and a low‑calorie finish.
instructions: In a bowl, combine chicken, onion, garlic, lemon juice, tamarind paste, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper. Toss until all pieces are coated and let marinate for 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the marinated chicken mixture and cook, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is no longer pink on the edges, about 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the chicken to become tender.
Uncover, add a splash of water (about 2 tbsp) to keep the dish moist, and cook uncovered for another 5 minutes, letting the sauce thicken slightly.
Transfer the tagine to a serving dish, and scatter the toffee popcorn evenly over the top for a sweet, crisp contrast.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro just before serving for freshness and color.
Serve hot with a side of steamed cauliflower rice or a small portion of whole‑wheat couscous if desired.
